title: salvia rufula subsp. latens
intro: Salvia rufula subsp. latens is a subspecies of the Salvia rufula plant, a perennial herb native to Mexico. It belongs to the Lamiaceae family and is also known as the "red sage" due to its reddish-purple flowers. The medicinal properties of Salvia rufula subsp. latens lie in its leaves, which contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, and have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Salvia rufula subsp. latens include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and support overall well-being.
